recipes.name: mitarbeiter-in-gruppe-aufnehmen
recipes.goal: Add an existing employee/client to a named group for a given period.

steps.prompt: Ask the user which group the employee should be added to (the group name).
steps.description: the name of the group the employee should be added to

steps.note: Find the target group. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching group automatically when exactly one group matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the employee (client) to add to the group.
steps.description: the full name (first and last name) of the employee to add

steps.note: Find the employee. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching employee automatically when exactly one matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the group membership should be valid (valid-until is optional).
steps.description: the start date from which the group membership is valid

steps.note: Add the employee to the group now. clientId and groupId are supplied — do not ask for them. Use the start date the user gave (see the known values below) as validFrom, formatted as a date (yyyy-MM-dd). This skill rejects duplicates, so it safely refuses if the employee is already in the group.

recipes.name: abwesenheit-fuer-mitarbeiter-eintragen
recipes.goal: Place an absence (Break) on an existing employee for a given date and absence type.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the full name (first and last name) of the employee the absence is for.
steps.description: the full name (first and last name) of the employee the absence is for

steps.note: Find the employee. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching employee automatically when exactly one matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user what kind of absence it is (e.g. vacation, sick, training, unpaid leave).
steps.description: the kind of absence (vacation, sick, training, ...)

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the date of the absence.
steps.description: the date of the absence as a calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd)

steps.note: List the available absence types so their IDs are known for the next step. Do not ask the user anything here; just call list_absence_types and read its result.

Place the absence now. clientId is supplied — do not ask for it. Choose absenceId from the absence types that list_absence_types just returned by matching the kind of absence the user named (see known values below). Use the date the user gave (known values) as date, formatted yyyy-MM-dd. Leave startTime, endTime and workTime at their defaults unless the user explicitly specified them.

recipes.name: neue-gruppe-erstellen
recipes.goal: Create a new group (root level or nested under a parent) with a name, validity start date and holiday calendar.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the new group.
steps.description: the name of the new group

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the new group is valid (valid-until is optional).
steps.description: the start date from which the new group is valid

steps.prompt: Ask the user where the group should sit: at the top level (root), or nested under an existing parent group. If nested, ask for the parent group's name.
steps.description: where the group belongs: 'root' / top level, or the name of the parent group to nest it under

steps.prompt: Ask the user which holiday calendar the group should use (e.g. a canton like 'Kanton Zürich').
steps.description: the name of the holiday calendar for the group (e.g. a canton)

steps.note: Find the holiday calendar. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching calendar automatically when exactly one matches.

Look up candidate parent groups for the placement the user gave. Just call list_groups and read its result; do not ask anything. If the placement is 'root' / top level, this lookup simply finds nothing relevant — that is fine.

Create the group now. name and calendarId are supplied — do not ask for them. Use the start date the user gave (see the known values below) as validFrom, formatted yyyy-MM-dd. PLACEMENT: if the user chose root / top level / 'Wurzel', or did NOT name an existing parent group, do NOT pass parentId (the group is created at root level). ONLY if the user named an existing parent group, set parentId to that group's Id from the list_groups result above. The payment interval defaults to Monthly.

recipes.name: dienst-in-gruppe-aufnehmen
recipes.goal: Add an existing shift/order to a named group.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the shift/order to add to the group.
steps.description: the name of the shift/order to add to the group

steps.note: Find the shift. The search string is supplied. The recipe captures the matching shift automatically when exactly one shift matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the group the shift should be added to.
steps.description: the name of the group the shift should be added to

steps.note: Find the target group. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching group automatically when exactly one group matches.
Add the shift to the group now. shiftId and groupId are supplied — do not ask for them. This skill rejects duplicates, so it safely refuses if the shift is already in the group.

recipes.name: kunde-zu-naechster-gruppe
recipes.goal: Add a customer to the group geographically nearest to the customer's address.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the customer to place into the nearest group.
steps.description: the name (or company) of the customer to place into the nearest group

steps.note: Find the customer. Pass the supplied search term AND entityType='Customer'. The recipe captures the matching customer automatically when exactly one matches.

Place the customer into the geographically nearest group now. clientId is supplied — do not ask for it. The nearest group is derived from group coordinates (geocoded from group names). If the customer has no geocoded address or no group carries coordinates, the skill leaves it unchanged and explains why — relay that explanation.

recipes.name: externen-mitarbeiter-zu-naechster-gruppe
recipes.goal: Add an external employee to the group geographically nearest to their address.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the external employee to place into the nearest group.
steps.description: the full name of the external employee to place into the nearest group

steps.note: Find the external employee. Pass the supplied search term AND entityType='ExternEmp'. The recipe captures the matching external employee automatically when exactly one matches.

Place the external employee into the geographically nearest group now. clientId is supplied — do not ask for it. The nearest group is derived from group coordinates (geocoded from group names). If the person has no geocoded address or no group carries coordinates, the skill leaves it unchanged and explains why — relay that explanation.
